plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETFs and remove below-stop positions.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad exposure.
Add to QQQ, targeting 20%, to enhance broad market exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
These moves align with capital preservation and a large cash buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative approach aims for ~1.5%/month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns. Expect minimal volatility but also limited upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to remove below-stop and leveraged positions.
Buy ANET and NOW, targeting 8% and 7% respectively, for high-score quality tech exposure.
Increase QQQI to 22% for a balanced income component.
This plan reduces overall leverage while adding quality growth names.
Maintain a cash buffer of ~10%.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month with a risk-first growth mindset. It reduces leverage and diversifies into strong scanner setups. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns, balancing growth with risk management.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit below-stop positions and free up capital.
Buy TECL, targeting 10%, to add leveraged tech exposure.
Initiate positions in ARM and NOW, targeting 10% each, for high-scoring growth.
This plan leans into strong tech setups and uses leveraged ETFs for aggressive growth.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer (~5%) with strict stop discipline.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, seeking stretched growth. It utilizes leveraged ETFs and concentrates in high-scoring setups. Be aware of significantly higher expected drawdowns and the daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged ETFs; strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETFs.
Increase QQQI to 30% to significantly boost income distributions.
Trim QQQ to 10% and SMH to 5% to reduce growth and sector concentration.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, focusing on reliable monthly distributions. It rotates heavily into income/covered-call ETFs and significantly reduces high-beta names. While prioritizing low drawdown, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs; this is not a savings account.
